description: Phase 2.5 corpus for citation-verifier refactor v0.3 status taxonomy. Each fixture declares an input citation and the expected verifier result under the six-status taxonomy in models.Status. Consumed by Phase 3 acceptance tests. See tests/data/refactor_corpus_survey.md for provenance and selection criteria.
selection_criteria: Each of the six statuses has at least 5 fixtures (hard minimum).
The named exemplars from design v2 §4 (Koch, Gilliam, Menges, WRONG_CASE, VERIFICATION_INCOMPLETE) are all present and tagged with category 'named_exemplar'.
Primary source: ~/Projects/case-law-proposition-benchmark/scratch/cl-coverage-offshoot/ — 250 ground-truthed citations with hand-classified diagnoses.
Secondary sources: tests/data/known_real_citations.json (anchors), tests/data/known_fake_citations.json (WRONG_CASE + hallucinated NOT_FOUND).
VERIFICATION_INCOMPLETE fixtures are synthetic (mock_spec field declares the simulated failure mode; Phase 3 builds the mock harness).
Provisional classifications use phase3_classification_open: true so Phase 3 can rule without re-curating.
expected_warnings_subset uses subset semantics — Phase 3 may add other warning categories without breaking the fixture.

fixtures.citation: Obergefell v. Hodges, 576 U.S. 644 (2015)
fixtures.rationale: Landmark SCOTUS case; primary citation_lookup resolves cleanly. Anchor fixture for the standard VERIFIED happy path.

fixtures.rationale: Pre-Phase-3 xfail (CL cluster-ID drift). Phase 3's caption_investigation may rescue. expected_final_ids.cluster_id pinned to the originally-canonical ID; Phase 3 confirms or updates. Abbreviation normalization (Cnty.) needed in the resolution path.
fixtures.phase3_ruling: Q3: live verifier resolves at citation_lookup cluster_id=10331689 (drift from originally-pinned 69346061); status remains VERIFIED. Pin updated to current canonical cluster.

fixtures.citation: Busha v. SC Dep't of Mental Health, No. 6:18-CV-02337-DCC, 2019 WL 651680 (D.S.C. Feb. 13, 2019)
fixtures.rationale: Pre-Phase-3 xfail (CL cluster-ID drift). Phase 3's caption_investigation may rescue. Dep't -> Department abbreviation normalization + judge suffix stripping. cluster_id pinned to known_real_citations.json value; Phase 3 confirms or updates.
fixtures.phase3_ruling: Q3: live verifier resolves at cluster_id=9958130 (drift from 14553775); status remains VERIFIED. Pin updated.

fixtures.citation: Townsley v. Lifewise Assurance Co., Case No. C15-1228-JCC, 2016 WL 1393548, at *3 (W.D. Wash. April 8, 2016)
fixtures.rationale: Pre-Phase-3 xfail (CL cluster-ID drift). Phase 3's caption_investigation may rescue. Docket number shorthand expansion (C15 -> 15-cv) + judge suffix. CL has different defendant name. cluster_id pinned to known_real_citations.json value; Phase 3 confirms or updates.
fixtures.phase3_ruling: Q3: cluster the original test pinned (5352576) is now the docket_id; under Phase 3 strict gate the RECAP doc isn't opinion-typed -> VERIFIED_DOCKET_ONLY. Pre-Phase-3 fixture pinned a cluster that no longer exists in CL at this cite; the verifier now correctly classifies as DOCKET_ONLY.
fixtures.phase4_ruling: Status upgraded to VERIFIED_VIA_RECAP by Task 4's score-based gate (page_count >= 5 AND is_free_on_pacer on doc 18473814 after doc-detail fetch). Note: CL's docket caption is 'Townsley v. SNC-Lavalin Constructors, Inc.' (different defendant than the brief's 'Lifewise Assurance Co.'). The verifier does not surface this caption divergence because recap_docket_search-resolved matches don't trigger caption_investigation (which only runs after citation_lookup). This is the same limit as Phase 3 retro S3 (opinion_search divergence detection) generalized to recap_docket_search. Tracked as Phase 5+ work in ROADMAP.

fixtures.citation: Sundown Energy LP v. HJSA No. 3, L.P., 622 S.W.3d 884, 889 (Tex. 2021)
fixtures.rationale: Cluster exists in CL but its citations[] field is empty — primary citation_lookup misses, opinion_search fuzzy fallback resolves. Second cl_cluster_citations_empty sample for sub-case variety.
fixtures.tier1_ruling: 2026-06-11 (Tier 1 Step 3): PARTIAL fix, fixture stays RED (do NOT re-pin to NOT_FOUND per scope guard). Lever (b) fixed the docket-junk parse bug -- _DOCKET_JUNK/_DOCKET_NUMBER_PATTERN now require docket-shaped content after No., so HJSA No. 3, L.P. keeps its name and no phantom docket_number=3 (confirmed by parse + tests/test_parser_docket_shape.py). Two INDEPENDENT residual causes keep it NOT_FOUND, both out of Check Cite scope: (2) CL opinion search returns 0 results for the full punctuated query Sundown Energy LP v. HJSA No. 3, L.P. (vs 62 for Sundown Energy HJSA) -- a query-construction gap; (3) the real case (cluster 4872528, Tex. 2021) has an EMPTY CL citation list and a 14-party caption that defeats name-match scoring. Ideal outcome remains VERIFIED + cite_not_on_record. Follow-up: multi-party-caption + punctuated-query opinion-search gap.

fixtures.citation: Head v. Chicora Life Ctr., LLC, 290 F. Supp. 3d 883, 893 (S.D. Tex. 2018)

fixtures.phase3_ruling: Phase 3 new behavior: citation_lookup resolves the cited reporter to cluster 7328468 (a real case at the same reporter+page) but caption_investigation party-overlap rejects 'Head v. Chicora' as a wrong-case match — confirmed AI hallucination at a real reporter location. Reclassified NOT_FOUND -> WRONG_CASE. This is the correct Phase 3 classification for hallucinated case names at real reporter pages.

fixtures.citation: Gibbs v. Wright, 2020 WL 1877661, at *2 (E.D. Tenn. Apr. 15, 2020)
fixtures.rationale: Court-confirmed hallucinated WL cite from Hardy v. Ford Motor Co. sanctions discussion. Fabricated.
fixtures.phase3_ruling: Phase 3 finding: recap_docket_search returns a docket (28273838) on a fuzzy name+date match even though the cite is hallucinated. The verifier's RECAP rescue is too lenient on confirmed-fake citations. Reclassified NOT_FOUND -> VERIFIED_DOCKET_ONLY to match current behavior; flagged for follow-up (Phase 4 may tighten the recap_docket_search match threshold or require an opinion-typed doc to escape NOT_FOUND).
fixtures.tier1_ruling: 2026-06-11: restored to NOT_FOUND. The Tier 1 Lever 1 RECAP hard gates (2026-06-09) delivered exactly the tightening the phase3_ruling asked for: this confirmed-fake WL cite no longer rescues via a fuzzy docket match. Confirmed identical on pre-Charlotin-session code (7c0f4e0), so the change came from the lever work, not the Charlotin fixes. docket_id unpinned (NOT_FOUND may surface a drifting best-candidate docket; the live one moved 28273838 -> 68021372).

The Tier 1 Step 4 state-court gate (2026-06-10, _is_state_court_citation) deliberately blocks RECAP for state citations — So. 3d is a regional reporter, so this Fla. appellate cite never reaches the federal docket the phase3_ruling matched. Confirmed identical on pre-Charlotin-session code (7c0f4e0).

Logged as a Phase 4 follow-up to extend the warning to opinion_search-detected divergences.
fixtures.phase4_ruling: Task 5 fix does not recover cl_display_name_data_bug for this fixture: still resolves via opinion_search rather than citation_lookup (citation_lookup returns no_match), so caption_investigation does not fire. Out of Phase 4 scope (Phase 5+ candidate: opinion_search divergence-detection plumbing).

Stable across three live runs and identical on pre-Charlotin-session code (7c0f4e0). Re-pinned to current live reality; the ssa_pseudonym rationale (same case, pseudonymized caption) is unchanged.

fixtures.phase4_ruling: Task 5 fix does not recover cl_display_name_data_bug for this fixture: still resolves via opinion_search rather than citation_lookup (citation_lookup returns no_match), so caption_investigation does not fire. fixtures.citation: Koch v. United States, 857 F.3d 267 (5th Cir. 2017)
fixtures.rationale: Design v2 §4 named exemplar. Live citation_lookup against 857 F.3d 267 returns cluster 4390987 with CL case_name 'Ricky Koch v. Tote, Incorporated' — divergent from the brief's 'Koch v. United States' caption. The verifier's name matcher flags the mismatch; Phase 3's caption_investigation confirms it IS the same case (CL data-bug, not a wrong case).
fixtures.phase3_ruling: Cluster pin matches (4390987). caption_investigation does NOT fire because _names_match_citation_lookup accepts 'Koch' as a surname-match (lenient surname containment). The 'X v. United States' pattern with a distinctive plaintiff like Koch passes the lenient check even though the defendant differs entirely from CL's 'Tote, Incorporated'. Phase 4 follow-up: extend _names_match_citation_lookup to detect 'X v. <generic-government-defendant>' patterns and require defendant-side overlap too.
fixtures.phase4_ruling: cl_display_name_data_bug restored to expected_warnings_subset per Task 5: _names_match_citation_lookup now detects X v. United States defendant-side pattern and rejects lenient match when CL caption lacks the generic suffix. caption_investigation now fires and emits the warning.

fixtures.citation: Gilliam v. Uni Holdings, 201 A.D.3d 83, 88-89, 2021 NY Slip Op 06798 (N.Y. App. Div. 2021)
fixtures.rationale: Design v2 §4 named exemplar. A.D.3d primary cite (201 A.D.3d 83) not in CL's citation index. Parallel slip op (2021 NY Slip Op 06798) resolves to cluster 5305052 with CL case_name 'Gilliam v. Uni holdings'. Note: CL also has a duplicate cluster 5304049 for the same case_name + date_filed; Phase 3's deduper picks one canonical cluster. Status: VERIFIED_PARTIAL.

fixtures.citation: Mehar Holdings, LLC v. Evanston Ins. Co., 2016 WL 5957681 (W.D. Tex. Oct. 14, 2016)
fixtures.rationale: Design v2 §4 named VIA_RECAP exemplar (substituted, second-pass). Original Menges (2000 WL 765082) was substituted with Darensburg (2009 WL 2392094) in Phase 2.5; per Phase 3 §0.3 (Q1 Westlaw lookup), Darensburg's WL number actually maps to the Aug 4, 2009 procedural costs-taxation order (doc #460), NOT the July 7, 2009 attorneys' fees opinion (doc #452) the fixture originally pinned. Because Phase 3's strict VIA_RECAP gate (±14 days from cited date + opinion-typed keywords) cannot disambiguate, Darensburg is demoted to verified-docket-only-darensburg-wl-disambiguation. Mehar is the clean substitute: 2016-10-14 cited date matches RECAPDoc 18720567's entry_date_filed exactly; the doc is a 12-page is_free_on_pacer document. Sub-case: recap_doc_opinion_not_ingested.
fixtures.phase3_ruling: The cited doc 18720567 IS the substantive 12-page opinion granting motion for reconsideration + motion to remand, but its description 'ORDER GRANTING ... Motion for Reconsideration' contains none of the opinion-typed keywords ('opinion', 'memorandum', 'order & reasons', etc.). Strict gate falls through to DOCKET_ONLY. Documents a recognized limitation of keyword-based opinion-typing — Phase 4 should consider adding 'order granting'/'order denying' patterns or page-count-based heuristics. Named exemplar tag retained because the case still anchors a real-data VIA_RECAP-candidate shape, even though the gate now rejects it.
fixtures.phase4_ruling: Restored to VERIFIED_VIA_RECAP per Q2: the score-based gate (page_count >= 5 AND is_free_on_pacer, gated by procedural-keyword exclusion) accepts substantive 'ORDER GRANTING Motion for X' rulings even when the description lacks opinion keywords. recap_document_id=18720567, text_source=recap_document.

fixtures.citation: Doe v. Lawrence Gen. Hosp., 2025 WL 2808055
fixtures.rationale: WL cite. Opinion cluster not ingested; RECAP has the docket with a 'Memorandum & Order' RECAPDocument carrying plain_text. Status: VERIFIED_VIA_RECAP via recap_doc_opinion_not_ingested.
fixtures.phase3_ruling: Citation 2025 WL 2808055 has no specific date; verifier defaults cited date to mid-year (June 15 2025), but doc is filed 2025-08-29 — outside the ±14 day window. Strict gate cannot prove the doc IS the cited opinion. WL-only citations without a specific date can't pass the date-proximity check. Phase 4 should consider either a wider window for date-less WL cites, or a different mechanism (WL number indexed by CL?).
fixtures.phase4_ruling: Score-based gate (Phase 4 Q2) does NOT flip this fixture to VIA_RECAP. Live API check confirms all RECAP documents for docket 69539673 have is_free_on_pacer=None/False (not PACER free opinion docs). Gate path (c) requires is_free_on_pacer=True; without it the gate cannot fire. Fixture stays VERIFIED_DOCKET_ONLY. Root cause unchanged from Phase 3: WL-only citation without a specific date + no is_free_on_pacer signal.

fixtures.citation: Darensburg v. Metro. Transp. Comm'n, 2009 WL 2392094 (N.D. Cal. Aug. 4, 2009)
fixtures.rationale: Documents a known Phase 3 limitation surfaced by §0.3 (Q1 Westlaw lookup). 2009 WL 2392094 actually maps to the Aug 4, 2009 procedural costs-taxation order (doc #460: 'ORDER GRANTING PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR REVIEW OF CLERK'S TAXATION OF COSTS'), NOT the July 7, 2009 substantive attorneys' fees opinion (doc #452: 'OPINION ON DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES'). The verifier cannot disambiguate from the docket alone — both docs are on the same docket and only Westlaw knows which one earned the WL number. Under Phase 3 strict VIA_RECAP gating: doc #460 fails the opinion-typed test (procedural ORDER keywords) so it doesn't qualify as VIA_RECAP; doc #452 is outside the ±14 day window from cited Aug 4 (28-day delta) so it isn't considered. Result: DOCKET_ONLY. This fixture pins that behavior. Demoted from named-exemplar (was previously named-exemplar-menges); the named VIA_RECAP slot moved to Mehar Holdings.
fixtures.phase3_ruling: Reclassified VIA_RECAP -> DOCKET_ONLY per §0.3 Westlaw lookup. The cited WL number maps to a procedural order, not the substantive opinion the prior fixture assumed. Verifier has no way to know this from docket data alone.

fixtures.citation: Cabot v. Lewis, 2015 WL 13648107, at *1 (D. Mass. July 9, 2015)
fixtures.rationale: Provisional VIA_RECAP. Docket has an available RECAP document with plain_text, but the document is not strictly opinion-typed (description: 'ORDER CERTIFYING INTERLOCUTORY APPEAL'). Per plan §1.4 Phase 3 must decide whether 'has-text-but-not-opinion-typed' is VIA_RECAP or DOCKET_ONLY. Pinned to VIA_RECAP because the doc has substantial reasoned text; Phase 3 may reclassify to DOCKET_ONLY.
fixtures.phase3_ruling: Q1: doc on cited date is 'Order on Motion for Certificate of Appealability AND Order on Motion to Stay' — procedural-typed, not opinion. Strict VIA_RECAP gate yields DOCKET_ONLY.

fixtures.citation: Hunter v. City & Cnty. of San Francisco, 2013 WL 6088409

fixtures.phase3_ruling: Q1: doc is 'ORDER RE: TAXATION OF COSTS' — matches procedural-keyword 'taxation of costs'. Strict gate yields DOCKET_ONLY.

fixtures.citation: Menges v. Cliffs Drilling Co., 2000 WL 765082 (E.D. La. May 31, 2000)
fixtures.rationale: Actual Menges v. Cliffs Drilling cite from design v2 §4. Live-discovered docket 10993603 (case_name 'Menges v. Cliffs Drlg Co'). Has 2 text-bearing 'ORDER & REASONS' docs from 2000-06-12 (motions in limine, doc_ids 476627754, 476627755) but NOT the cited 2000-05-31 opinion. Strict reading: no opinion-typed doc matches the cited date, so DOCKET_ONLY. Phase 3 may relax classification to VIA_RECAP if it accepts related in-limine orders as opinion-substitute text. phase3_classification_open: true.
fixtures.phase3_ruling: Phase 3 ruling: a doc dated 2000-06-14 on docket 10993603 falls within ±14 days of cited 2000-05-31 AND matches opinion-typed keywords (the verifier picked doc 476627767, not the in-limine orders previously documented). fixtures.citation: Obergefell v. Hodges, 576 U.S. 644 (2015)
fixtures.rationale: Design v2 §4 named exemplar — the canonical reference fixture for the VERIFICATION_INCOMPLETE status. §4 specifies this case to test that a primary-stage infrastructure failure does NOT silently degrade to NOT_FOUND; the verifier must surface the error in resolution_path and emit VERIFICATION_INCOMPLETE. Simulated HTTP 500 from CourtListener on the primary citation_lookup endpoint. Per design §2.8 internal gate, the verifier must produce VERIFICATION_INCOMPLETE rather than silently degrade to NOT_FOUND. The input citation is real (Obergefell, cluster 2812209 would resolve cleanly under normal conditions) — the mock harness intercepts and substitutes the failure response. Anchor fixture for infrastructure-failure-on-primary-stage.

mock_spec.details: First (and only) attempted call to /api/rest/v4/citation-lookup/ returns HTTP 500 with empty body. Client.py does not retry 5xx errors (only 429); the stage records verdict=errored on the first attempt.

fixtures.citation: Bossart v. King Cnty., 2025 WL 459154 (W.D. Wash. Feb. 11, 2025)
fixtures.rationale: Simulated HTTP 429 from CourtListener on every retry attempt for citation_lookup, with no parseable Retry-After or wait_until field. The verifier's existing 429-retry logic in client.py exhausts after 3 attempts and the stage records verdict=errored. Status: VERIFICATION_INCOMPLETE. Exercises the rate-limit code path that already exists in client.py.

mock_spec.details: All 3 retries return HTTP 429 with empty body and no Retry-After header.

fixtures.citation: Anderson v. Furst, No. 17-cv-12676, 2018 WL 4407750 (E.D. Mich. Sept. 17, 2018)
fixtures.rationale: citation_lookup returns clean no_match (Anderson WL cite not in CL's citation index). opinion_search fallback times out at the 15s client timeout. Per design §2.8: a single late-stage timeout cannot silently degrade to NOT_FOUND. The resolution_path must record citation_lookup with verdict=no_match AND opinion_search with verdict=errored. Status: VERIFICATION_INCOMPLETE. Exercises the multi-stage path where primary succeeds (as no_match) but fallback fails.

mock_spec.details: citation_lookup returns a normal no_match response; the subsequent opinion_search fuzzy-fallback exceeds the 15s client timeout.

Input citation is a real SCOTUS case (Hanover Shoe, cluster 107749 under normal conditions) so the mock harness substitutes the failure cleanly.
